title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder in stabbing of Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez, 25, in Chelsea.
The stabbing occurred on March 19 near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, captured by city cameras.
Castellanos Cerrato, arraigned and held without bail, due back in court April 21; police seek public information.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston metro area's population growth in 2025 was the slowest since 2021, adding just 11,991 people.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,524 in 2025, the smallest increase since the pandemic's onset.
Middlesex and Worcester counties had the highest increases but significantly lower than in 2024.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod-based researchers launched a GoFundMe to raise $15,000 for North Atlantic right whale conservation efforts.
The Marineland Right Whale Project needs donations for fieldwork, including aerial surveys and specialized equipment.
North Atlantic right whales face critical threats from vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements, with only a few hundred remaining.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in his first season after being traded from the Raiders.
Wes Welker became a prolific slot receiver for the Patriots after being acquired from the Miami Dolphins in 2007.
Josh Gordon showed potential with over 700 yards in 11 games in 2018 but was hindered by off-field issues.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected for arguing an inning-ending strike three call against Trevor Story in a 6-5 loss to the Reds.
Cora did not criticize umpire C.B. Bucknor after the game, acknowledging that mistakes happen and emphasizing the human element of umpiring.
Roman Anthony regretted using the team's final ABS challenge early, leaving the Red Sox unable to contest later disputed calls.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call during Red Sox's 6-5 loss, leading to manager Alex Cora's ejection.
Story hit the Red Sox's first home run of 2026, but his controversial strikeout was a pivotal moment in the game.
Boston tied the game with Wilyer Abreu's home run, but ultimately lost 6-5 in the 11th inning to fall to 1-1 this season.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games since returning from the Olympic break.
An ill-timed concussion prevented Zacha from joining Czechia at the Milano-Cortina Olympics, fueling his post-break performance.
Zacha, acquired from New Jersey for Erik Haula, is having a career year with 26 goals and nearing 60 points.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray struggled in his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds, lasting four innings in a 6-5 loss.
Gray allowed four runs across the first three innings, including a solo home run by Sal Stewart in the third.
Manager Alex Cora praised Gray's resilience despite the rocky start and noted the bullpen's strong performance.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 participants attended the third 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common as part of a nationwide demonstration.
Key speakers included Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, addressing topics like elections, military actions, and immigration.
The event featured performances by Dropkick Murphys and Good Trouble Brass Band, with multiple advocacy group sign-up tables available.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute, 17-song set at the House of Blues, featuring theatrical shock rock elements.
The concert included a cover of Chappell Roan's "Pink Pony Club" and elaborate costumes with fake blood sprays.
The band incorporated social and political satire, targeting figures like Donald Trump and Kristi Noem in their performance.
